remember: Students will be able to recall the key events and milestones in the Indian freedom struggle.
Students will be able to identify the major leaders and their contributions to the freedom movement.
Students will be able to list the various forms of resistance and protests used by Indians against British rule.
understand: Students will be able to describe the social, economic, and political factors that led to the growth of the freedom movement.
Students will be able to explain the role of the Indian National Congress in the freedom struggle.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of the Non-Cooperation Movement and the Quit India Movement on the British government.
apply: Students will be able to evaluate the strengths and weaknesses of different approaches to achieving freedom, such as non-violent resistance and armed struggle.
Students will be able to assess the role of key individuals, such as Mahatma Gandhi and Subhas Chandra Bose, in the freedom movement.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the freedom movements in India and other countries.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the complex interplay of social, economic, and political factors that contributed to the success of the freedom movement.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of the freedom movement on Indian society and politics.
Students will be able to assess the significance of the freedom movement in the context of India's modern history.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the factors that led to the growth of the Indian National Congress and its role in the freedom movement.
To analyze the impact of the Non-Cooperation Movement and the Quit India Movement on the British government and Indian society.
To evaluate the contributions of key leaders, such as Mahatma Gandhi and Subhas Chandra Bose, to the freedom movement.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the major causes of the Indian freedom struggle and its key milestones.
To describe the role of the Indian National Congress in the freedom movement and its significance in Indian history.
To analyze the impact of the freedom movement on Indian society and politics.
